title: Report: Alaska military sites vulnerable to climate change impacts
summary: The U.S. Department of Defense has 550,000 facilities covering 28 million acres, with an estimated value of about $850 billion, which means it could see big costs associated with the impacts of climate change, according to a new report from the Government Accountability Office.

title: Flood control keeps water off Fairbanks streets yet again
summary: The most effective flood insurance policy in Fairbanks can be found 17 miles east of the city, about 35 river miles along the meandering Chena River. It takes the form of an unusual dam with four 30-ton gates that operate like giant garage doors, stemming the flow of high water when the river rises.

title: Retirement bill, meant to offset costs, will actually increase unfunded liability, actuaries say
summary: A state retirement bill signed and praised by Gov. Sean Parnell on Monday will increase the state's unfunded liability and increase costs years into the future, actuaries told the Alaska Retirement Management Board this week.

title: Benghazi suspect appears in U.S. courtrooom
summary: The man charged with participating in the lethal attack on the U.S. consulate in Benghazi made his first appearance in a U.S. courtroom Saturday afternoon.

title: Election officials gave less language help to Natives outside Bethel, court told
summary: Alaska election officials made less of an effort to expand the reach of their Yup'ik language assistance program to regions beyond Bethel because it wasn't required under the settlement of a 2007 lawsuit, elections division director Gail Fenumiai testified Friday.

title: Government aims to seize Anchorage properties of man allegedly involved in $1B international laundering scheme
summary: A businessman and his family could lose more than 20 Anchorage properties that federal investigators believe were paid for using money he received for illegal business dealings involving the government of Iran.

title: State: FDA study finds Alaska fish safe from Fukushima radiation
summary: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration has found that Alaska seafood is safe from Fukushima radiation, but a citizens group plans to conduct a separate study of the water in lower Cook Inlet using a crowdsource funding site.

title: Talkeetna's Historic Fairview Inn for sale
summary: For nearly a century, Talkeetna's Fairview Inn has served as a mecca for locals and travelers alike. Now, for the first time in decades, the historic structure is for sale.

title: Man charged with fatal shooting told police he found his girlfriend's body with the gun
summary: Breanna Richelle Moore's boyfriend, Joshua Almeda, told police he found her dead with a gun. But police said "evidence at the scene was not consistent with suicide."

title: Man charged with fatal shooting told police he found gun with girlfriend's body
summary: Breanna Richelle Moore's boyfriend, Joshua Almeda, told police he found her dead with a gun. But police said evidence at the scene was not consistent with suicide.

title: Don Young's fondness for hunting at game ranches detailed in House Ethics documents
summary: While Rep. Don Young may be familiar with the bog-slogging and bug-swatting Alaska experience in home-state hunts for moose and caribou, that's not the description of his hunting trips contained in the House Ethics report that rebuked him last week.

title: Russians target State Dept. spokeswoman for media assault
summary: Among the Russian bloggers and social media activists who are loyal to the Kremlin, there’s a favorite target when it comes to pillorying the United States.

title: Homeland Security chief defends agency's handling of youth at border
summary: The nation’s top homeland security official on Tuesday defended his department’s handling of unaccompanied children crossing the U.S. border from Mexico, turning aside Republican demands for a National Guard presence there even as officials predict the surge will continue into next year.

title: As frozen debris field slides toward Dalton Highway, state makes plans to move road
summary: The state plans to move a 2,000-foot section of the Dalton Highway in 2017, hoping to delay a collision of geologic proportions.

title: Investigative committee subpoenas White House lawyer over IRS emails
summary: Less than an hour before Internal Revenue Service Commissioner John Koskinen was scheduled to testify before the House Committee on Oversight and Government Reform Monday in a rare night hearing, the committee chairman announced a subpoena to compel testimony from a White House lawyer.

title: French traveler known for taking risks missing in Southwest Alaska
summary: Last fall, an adventurous French man named Francois Guenot appeared in villages dotting the shores of Lake Iliamna. He'd apparently paddled across Cook Inlet in a makeshift kayak. Now, after setting out on another adventure, Guenot is missing -- with only an empty kayak, a waterproof bag and a final journal entry to suggest his last location.

title: As traffic grows, Whittier tunnel flourishes during summer months
summary: Last year, the Anton Anderson Memorial Tunnel that connects Whittier to the rest of the world by road brought in more than $1.7 million, just $200,000 less than its peak in 2005. And traffic has ramped up by nearly 40 percent since 2001.

title: Candidate profile: Treadwell's underdog run for US Senate draws on 40 years of Alaska work
summary: Lt. Gov. Mead Treadwell, once considered a moderate Republican, is branding himself as the true Alaskan conservative in a fierce political contest for U.S. Senate.

title: Judge gives Rumrunners owners maximum fine in assault case
summary: The corporation behind the now-defunct downtown nightspot Rumrunners Old Towne Bar and Grille and a former bouncer were sentenced Friday in Anchorage Superior Court, each for a single count of misdemeanor assault. Both defendants were found guilty in February.

title: Alaska Rep. Don Young rebuked by ethics committee
summary: Rep. Don Young has been rebuked by the House Ethics Committee for using campaign funds for personal trips to hunting lodges, accepting gifts of travel and lodging in violation of House rules, and failure to report gifts on his financial disclosure reports.

title: Alaska's Don Young rebuked by ethics committee
summary: The House Ethics Committee said Friday that Rep. Don Young violated Congressional rules by improperly accepting nearly $60,000 in hunting trips, rides on private planes and other gifts and failing to report them on his financial disclosure forms.

title: State: Alaska's same-sex marriage ban doesn't violate couples' rights
summary: Five couples suing to overturn Alaskas ban on same-sex marriage have had none of their constitutional rights violated and thus no legal justification for their goal, the state argued in a document filed in federal court on Thursday.

title: Alaska police chiefs say legalizing marijuana will increase funding, training needs
summary: The Alaska Association of Chiefs of Police says local police departments could end up spending $6 million next year if a ballot initiative to legalize marijuana passes in November.

title: Authorities reduce search efforts for missing Kenai family
summary: Kenai police continue to send out updates on their search for a missing Kenai family of four. On Wednesday, they offered a single new detail about how the one of the missing family members spent his free time.

title: Frustrations mount as king salmon conservation takes priority on the Kuskokwim River
summary: Villagers in the Kuskokwim region are concerned they won't be able to harvest fish this season as managers of the fishery impose harsh restrictions on king salmon.

title: Alaska looking for portable morgue trailers
summary: Alaska looking for portable morgue trailers

title: Probable case of paralytic shellfish poisoning reported at Kenai Peninsula's Clam Gulch
summary: A Kenai resident reported classic symptoms of paralytic shellfish poisoning -- including tingling of the mouth, shortness of breath and vomiting -- after eating razor and possibly butter clams harvested from Kenai Peninsula's Clam Gulch on Sunday, according to state health officials.

title: Strange 'swarm' of earthquakes near Noatak confounds scientists, frightens residents
summary: A magnitude 5.7 earthquake early this morning near Noatak marks the fifth quake of exactly that size measured around the northwest Alaska village since April, according to the Alaska Earthquake Information Center.

title: Despite $3 billion boost, Alaska pension cost will hit municipalities hard
summary: While a $3 billion payout from savings shores up the retirement systems for Alaska's public employees, the Legislature's action also shifted more of the costs onto municipalities across the state.

title: Coming soon to Alaska bars: Free pregnancy tests
summary: Coming soon to Alaska bars: Free pregnancy tests

title: Border patrol chief: No time line on investigations
summary: There is no time line for investigations into allegations of abuse by U.S. border authorities against unaccompanied minors because of a lack of specificity in the complaints, the head of U.S. Customs and Border Protection said Friday.

title: Palmer 'rain garden' is scenic sponge for polluted runoff
summary: The demonstration garden in Palmer is part of a movement to treat pollutant-carrying storm water runoff and keep it out of rivers or lakes to safeguard water quality, project coordinators say.

title: Minors at border will go to Health and Human Services
summary: A surge of unaccompanied children crossing the United States border in southwest Texas are not eligible for any amnesty or path to citizenship and are being transferred to the custody of the federal Health and Human Services Department, said U.S. Secretary of Homeland Security Jeh Johnson at a news conference Thursday.

title: AK Beat: Groups file suit hoping for protections for Southeast Alaska wolf
summary: Two environmental groups and a small tour-boat company on Tuesday sued the U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service over the agencys failure to grant Endangered Species Act protections to the Alexander Archipelago wolf.

title: Kenai police, FBI continue search for family that disappeared under 'mysterious circumstances'
summary: The FBI said Monday it became involved in the investigation of a missing Kenai family due to the young ages of the children involved and the fact that the family disappeared under mysterious circumstances. Also on Monday, Adams' sister recounted her last conversation with the missing mother.

title: White House: Fathers need help to stay at home, too
summary: Normally, a baseball player gets invited to the White House by winning the World Series. New York Mets second baseman Daniel Murphy did it Monday by missing the first two games of this season.

title: Wells Fargo to repatriate traditional Dena'ina land
summary: A 143-acre tract of land near Eklutna will be transferred from the bank to a nonprofit affiliated with the local Native corporation, Eklutna Inc.

title: Five students graduate from Dental Health Aide Therapists program, marking 10th anniversary
summary: Five students graduated Friday from the Dental Health Aide Therapist program, marking the 10th anniversary of the program, which seeks to bring dental care to rural Alaska, where access is limited and dental health problems far surpass the national average.

title: New whistleblower group encourages more efforts to inform public
summary: A new whistleblower protection effort debuted this week, claiming that safeguards to shield employees who expose government activities from retaliation are not strong enough.

title: Alaska students who didn't pass exit exam can now get diplomas
summary: With the death of Alaska's HSGQE test, the thousands of students who have finished high school without passing the exam will be eligible to receive the diplomas that eluded them.

title: In Alaska bear encounters, could old advice be completely wrong?
summary: Despite the oft-repeated advice that you should never run from a bear, over the years Ive talked to dozens, possibly hundreds, of people who've run from bears. None were victims of maulings.

title: McGuire withdraws from race, contenders emerge for state seats
summary: Alaska Sen. Lesil McGuire said Monday she's withdrawing from the Republican primary for lieutenant governor in Alaska, citing concerns about how the race would interfere with her family life.

title: Musher Mitch Seavey settles with Oregon knife maker over 2011 Iditarod accident
summary: Iditarod musher Mitch Seavey and an Oregon knife company say they have settled a court fight over knife safety, avoiding a jury trial that was due to begin Monday. Details of the settlement were not announced.

title: Human remains discovered by crew battling Kenai Peninsula wildfire
summary: A crew working on the massive Funny River wildfire found unidentified human remains Sunday near Sterling, according to the Alaska State Troopers.

title: New Anchorage business provides health scans for cats, dogs, beluga whales
summary: After years of relying on equipment intended for human use, vets now have a dedicated animal-friendly facility where Alaskans can take their pets to get MRIs and CT scans.

title: Call him 'Minimum Mike' if you like, but this Barrow judge is trying something new
summary: If you are accused of a crime in Barrow, 320 miles above the Arctic Circle, and you can't walk, a wheelchair ramp leads to the swinging courthouse doors. Watch the ice.

title: Call him 'Minimum Mike' if you like, but this Barrow judge is trying something new
summary: If you are accused of a crime in Barrow, 320 miles above the Arctic Circle, and you can't walk, a wheelchair ramp leads to the swinging courthouse doors. Watch the ice.

title: 'Gunfire' just one indication 'Bush People' show rankles Copper River neighbors
summary: 'Gunfire' just one indication 'Bush People' show rankles Copper River neighbors

title: Drone lends a wing to help control Kenai Peninsula wildfire
summary: Over the vast expanse of the smoldering Kenai Peninsula wildfire, a little unmanned aircraft is working long hours picking up signs of hot spots and mapping fire boundaries.
